analysis.mistake: CPT block placeholders ended with ellipsis ('...'), name fields lost spacing when empty, first name field was not focusable when content was empty.
analysis.expected: Placeholders should be plain text without trailing punctuation. Space between first and last name fields should always be visible. All name fields should be focusable even when empty.
analysis.rootCause: Developer made reasonable but incorrect design interpretations: (1) assumed placeholder text should match form convention of ending with ellipsis, (2) used text-node spacer approach instead of flexbox gap for dual-field spacing, which became invisible when both fields were empty, (3) did not apply minimum width to RichText name fields, making them un-focusable when empty due to zero width.

suggestions.content: CPT block inline editor placeholders must be plain text without trailing ellipsis or punctuation (e.g., 'First name' not 'First name...'). Placeholder text should be concise and action-oriented.
suggestions.rationale: Ellipsis in placeholders creates redundant visual noise in CPT block cards. Plain text improves authoring clarity and matches WordPress block editor conventions.

suggestions.content: Dual inline RichText name fields must use flex gap-2 flex-wrap on the wrapper and inline-block min-w-[6rem] on each RichText component. This ensures the gap remains visible even when both fields are empty, and keeps both fields focusable for editing.
suggestions.rationale: Flexbox gap is always rendered, unlike text-node spacers which disappear when empty. Minimum width prevents zero-width fields that cannot receive focus.
